this is a reactjs antdesign component, those 2 columns aren't on the same line. fix it: " قم بالمهام البسيطة وتقاضى الأجر!

كل ما عليك فعله هو تقييم الخدمات التجارية عبر الإنترنت وسندفع لك المال مقابل كل تقييم تقوم به،
فقط قم بعمل حساب جديد ويمكنك البدء في تنفيذ المهام على الفور!

"

One possible solution is to add the class "d-flex" to the Row component and set the height of both columns to "100%" using the style property. Here is the updated code: قم بالمهام البسيطة وتقاضى الأجر!

كل ما عليك فعله هو تقييم الخدمات التجارية عبر الإنترنت وسندفع لك المال مقابل كل تقييم تقوم به،
فقط قم بعمل حساب جديد ويمكنك البدء في تنفيذ المهام على الفور!

Note that the "d-flex" class makes the Row component a flex container, which allows us to vertically align its children using the "align-items" property. Setting the height of the columns to "100%" ensures that they occupy the full height of the row. Also, we set the style property "marginTop: 'auto'" to the Button component to push it to the bottom of its column.
didn't work
how to force elements to stay on one line

To force elements to stay on one line, you can use the CSS property `white-space: nowrap` on their container. This will prevent them from wrapping to a new line and they will stay on the same line.

Here's an example using your code:

```
<Row className="hidden-sm row-flex" justify="space-around" style={{marginTop:'-180px', padding:'0 50px 0 50px', whiteSpace: 'nowrap'}}>
  <Col span={12}>
    ...
  </Col>
  <Col span={12}>
    ...
  </Col>
</Row>
```

I added the "whiteSpace" property to the Row style with a value of "nowrap". This should force both columns to stay on the same line.

Hope this helps!
not working

I apologize that the previous solutions didn't work for you. Here's another approach you can try:

Use `display: inline-block` for the columns to force them to appear on the same line.

```
<Row className="hidden-sm" justify="space-around" align="middle" style={{marginTop:'-180px', padding:'0 50px 0 50px', whiteSpace: 'nowrap'}}>
  <Col span={12} style={{ display: 'inline-block', verticalAlign: 'middle' }}>
    ...
  </Col>
  <Col span={12} style={{ display: 'inline-block', verticalAlign: 'middle' }}>
    ...
  </Col>
</Row>
```

In this example, I added the `display: inline-block` style rule to both columns to make them appear inline with each other, and I also added the `verticalAlign: 'middle'` style rule to center them horizontally.

I hope this helps!
write a professional "who are we" for a website called "كريبوتاسكي" the website makes you perform tasks for money, write a beloving paragraph in arabic.

نحن في "كريبوتاسكي" نعمل بشغف لتقديم فرص مناسبة وآمنة للأشخاص لتحقيق دخل إضافي وتطوير مهاراتهم في العمل والتقنية. نحن نؤمن بالابتكار والتجربة الجريئة، وبأن العمل هو وسيلة لتحقيق الرفاهية والتطور الشخصي. نحن ندعم مجتمعنا ونسعى لتوفير فرص عمل متنوعة ومجزية، ونحرص على توفير بيئة عمل آمنة وصديقة لجميع مستخدمينا. قيمنا الأساسية هي الصدق والعمل الجاد والتعاون؛ ونحن نضمن دائمًا التزامنا بتقديم خدمات عالية الجودة والاهتمام الفائق بتجربة المستخدم. شكرًا لكم على دعمكم لنا ونحن نتطلع إلى العمل معكم!
how to put a maximum characters for html paragraph

You can use the `text-overflow` and `overflow` CSS properties to limit the visible characters of an HTML paragraph element. Here's an example: ```html

This is a long paragraph that will be truncated after 50 characters to show an ellipsis.

``` In this example, the CSS properties used are: - `white-space: nowrap`: This prevents the text from wrapping to another line. - `overflow: hidden`: This hides any text that overflows outside the paragraph box. - `text-overflow: ellipsis`: This adds an ellipsis to the end of the paragraph to indicate that the text has been truncated. - `max-width: 200px`: This limits the paragraph width to a maximum of 200 pixels. You can adjust the values of `max-width` and the number of characters to be displayed before the ellipsis as per your requirement. I hope this helps!

i've a reactJS, antDesign project. I wanna click on a button and smoothly transition to an a href
I meant an onPage scrolling without external links
what if it's more than one button and more than one destination?

If you have multiple buttons that scroll to different sections of the page, you can use the `Link` component from `react-scroll` to create clickable links that automatically scroll to the desired section. Here's an example: ```jsx import React from 'react'; import { Button } from 'antd'; import { Link, Element } from 'react-scroll'; function App() { return (

Section 1

This is section 1.

Section 2

This is section 2.
); } export default App; ``` In this example, each button has its own `Link` component with a unique `to` prop that matches the `name` prop of the corresponding `Element` component. The `smooth` and `duration` props are used to create a smooth scrolling effect with a duration of 800ms. Make sure to give each `Element` component a unique `name` prop that matches the corresponding `to` prop in the `Link` component. I hope this helps!
